content.text:
<user_query>
I made a mistake. This project is goaling manage self hosted runners for GitHub workflows. It shouldn't be named `gh-wm`, it should be `gh-sr`. Help me to update through the codebase.
</user_query>

Searching the codebase for `gh-wm` and related identifiers to plan a consistent rename to `gh-sr`.

[REDACTED]

Having mapped references: the rename spans the Go module path, `cmd/gh-wm` â†’ `cmd/gh-sr`, the `gh wm` Cobra command â†’ `gh sr`, `~/.gh-wm`, `GH_WM_CONFIG`, and internal identifiers (`ghwm-*`, metrics markers, Docker helper paths). Creating the plan.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Rename gh-wm to gh-sr
input.overview: Rename the project from `gh-wm` / `gh wm` to `gh-sr` / `gh sr` consistently: Go module and imports, CLI entrypoint directory and binary name, user-facing config paths, environment variable, internal service/Docker/metrics identifiers, documentation, and build metadata. This is a breaking change for existing installs (config directory and env var).
input.plan:
# Rename project: `gh-wm` â†’ `gh-sr`

## Scope (what changes)

| Area | From | To |
|------|------|-----|
| Go module / imports | `github.com/an-lee/gh-wm` | `github.com/an-lee/gh-sr` |
| Entry package | [`cmd/gh-wm/`](cmd/gh-wm/) | `cmd/gh-sr/` (move/rename directory) |
| Built binary / Makefile `BINARY` | `gh-wm` | `gh-sr` ([`Makefile`](Makefile)) |
| GitHub CLI subcommand | `Use: "wm"` â†’ `gh wm` | `Use: "sr"` â†’ `gh sr` ([`cmd/gh-wm/main.go`](cmd/gh-wm/main.go)) |
| User config directory | `~/.gh-wm` | `~/.gh-sr` (all path helpers, strings, tests) |
| Config env var | `GH_WM_CONFIG` ([`internal/config/paths.go`](internal/config/paths.go)) | `GH_SR_CONFIG` |
| API helper | `WmDir()` | `SrDir()` (rename function + call sites in [`cmd/gh-wm/main.go`](cmd/gh-wm/main.go)) |
| Product strings | â€śGitHub Workflow Manager (gh wm)â€ť | Align with self-hosted runners, e.g. short description: manage self-hosted GitHub Actions runners; long text updated similarly |

## Internal identifiers (avoid leaving `wm`/`ghwm` in generated artifacts)

These appear in systemd units, Launchd plists, Windows tasks, Docker bootstrap scripts, and remote echo markersâ€”rename so new installs are unambiguous:

- **Autostart** ([`internal/autostart/generate.go`](internal/autostart/generate.go), [`autostart.go`](internal/autostart/autostart.go), [`generate_test.go`](internal/autostart/generate_test.go)): `ghwm-runner-*` â†’ `ghsr-runner-*`, `com.github.ghwm.runner.*` â†’ `com.github.ghsr.runner.*`, Description strings `(gh wm)` â†’ `(gh sr)`.
- **Docker Windows path** ([`internal/runner/docker.go`](internal/runner/docker.go), [`docker_test.go`](internal/runner/docker_test.go)): PowerShell vars `$ghwmDockerConfigDir` â†’ `$ghsrDockerConfigDir`, temp folder `'gh-wm-docker-config'` â†’ `'gh-sr-docker-config'`, stub registry key `"ghwm.invalid"` â†’ `"ghsr.invalid"`.
- **Native tarball prefix** ([`internal/runner/native.go`](internal/runner/native.go)): `ghwm-runner-` â†’ `ghsr-runner-` in temp artifact names; update [`native_test.go`](internal/runner/native_test.go) expectations.
- **Host metrics protocol** ([`internal/host/metrics.go`](internal/host/metrics.go), [`metrics_test.go`](internal/host/metrics_test.go)): markers `::GH_WM_METRICS_START::` / `END` â†’ `::GH_SR_METRICS_*::` (both shell and PowerShell paths).
- **Tests** ([`internal/config/config_test.go`](internal/config/config_test.go)): `GH_WM_TEST_PAT` â†’ `GH_SR_TEST_PAT`; default path test expects `.gh-sr/runners.yml`; message strings referencing `GH_WM_CONFIG` â†’ `GH_SR_CONFIG`.

## Files touched by string/path sweep

- **All `.go` files** with import path or user-visible `gh wm` / `.gh-wm` / `GH_WM` (grep already listed ~25 files): mechanical replace of imports, help text, and error hints.
- **Config templates**: [`internal/config/runners.yml.template`](internal/config/runners.yml.template), [`internal/config/template.go`](internal/config/template.go), [`config/runners.yml`](config/runners.yml).
- **Docs site**: [`README.md`](README.md), [`docs/hugo.toml`](docs/hugo.toml) (`baseURL`, `BookRepo`), and all under `docs/content/**/*.md` (large `gh wm` / `.gh-wm` / `GH_WM_CONFIG` / GitHub URLs).
- **Build / ignore**: [`Makefile`](Makefile) header comment and `CMD_DIR`; [`.gitignore`](.gitignore) entry `/gh-wm` â†’ `/gh-sr`.
- **[`.github/workflows/release.yml`](.github/workflows/release.yml)**: no path change if it only uses `go_version_file`; verify after `go.mod` update.

## Repo and GitHub (manual steps for you)

1. Rename the GitHub repository `an-lee/gh-wm` â†’ `an-lee/gh-sr` (GitHub redirect may help old links briefly).
2. Locally: rename workspace folder `gh-wm` â†’ `gh-sr` and `git remote set-url origin` to the new URL.
3. Republish docs: update GitHub Pages URL from `https://an-lee.github.io/gh-wm/` to `.../gh-sr/` in [`docs/hugo.toml`](docs/hugo.toml) and any Pages settings if needed.
4. **Users**: `gh extension install an-lee/gh-sr` after publish; existing `~/.gh-wm` is not migrated automaticallyâ€”document that they should move/rename to `~/.gh-sr` and switch `GH_SR_CONFIG` if used.

## Optional (not in default scope)

- Deprecate `GH_WM_CONFIG` by reading it if `GH_SR_CONFIG` is unset (backward compatibility)â€”only if you want a softer migration.

## Verification (after implementation)

- `go test ./...` from repo root.
- `make build` produces `gh-sr` (or `gh-sr.exe` on Windows per Makefile).
- Spot-check: `gh sr --help`, `gh sr config path` (default path under `~/.gh-sr`).

todos.content: Update go.mod module path; rename cmd/gh-wm to cmd/gh-sr; fix all import paths
Cobra Use sr, SrDir, GH_SR_CONFIG, ~/.gh-sr strings in paths.go, main.go, load.go
Rename ghwm/gh-wm identifiers in autostart, docker, native, metrics + tests
Sweep README, docs/, hugo.toml, templates, config/runners.yml, Makefile, .gitignore
Run go test ./... and make build

content.text:
Updating `paths.go` and applying bulk string replacements in `main.go` and `load.go`.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
Linux hosts: gh wm setup and update may run package installs and similar steps on the remote host. For a non-root
SSH user, gh wm uses sudo when the sudo binary exists on the remote PATH; SSH is non-interactive, so passwordless
input.new_string:
Linux hosts: gh sr setup and update may run package installs and similar steps on the remote host. For a non-root
SSH user, gh sr uses sudo when the sudo binary exists on the remote PATH; SSH is non-interactive, so passwordless

input.old_string:
Native runners do not survive host reboot until OS autostart is installed (gh wm service install). After install,
gh wm up and gh wm down start and stop the same supervisor (systemd, launchd, or a Windows scheduled task).
input.new_string:
Native runners do not survive host reboot until OS autostart is installed (gh sr service install). After install,
gh sr up and gh sr down start and stop the same supervisor (systemd, launchd, or a Windows scheduled task).

input.old_string: Docker mode uses the container restart policy unless-stopped; gh wm service install skips docker runners.`
input.new_string: Docker mode uses the container restart policy unless-stopped; gh sr service install skips docker runners.`

input.description: Bulk replace CLI strings and SrDir in main.go

input.old_string: Long: `GitHub Workflow Manager (gh sr) manages self-hosted GitHub Actions runners on any combination
input.new_string: Long: `Self-hosted runner manager for GitHub (gh sr) manages self-hosted GitHub Actions runners on any combination

input.description: Bulk rename wm/ghwm/GH_WM patterns in all Go files

input.old_string: const EnvFileTemplate = `# Optional dotenv-style variables for GitHub Workflow Manager (loaded before runners.yml).
input.new_string: const EnvFileTemplate = `# Optional dotenv-style variables for self-hosted runner manager (loaded before runners.yml).

todos.content: Cobra Use sr, SrDir, GH_SR_CONFIG, ~/.gh-sr strings in paths.go, main.go, load.go
Rename ghwm/gh-wm identifiers in autostart, docker, native, metrics + tests
Rename ghwm/gh-wm identifiers in autostart, docker, native, metrics + tests
Sweep README, docs/, hugo.toml, templates, config/runners.yml, Makefile, .gitignore

input.command:
cd /home/an-lee/projects/gh-wm && \
  find docs README.md Makefile .gitignore internal/config/runners.yml.template config/runners.yml -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r f; do \
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue; \
    sed -i \
      -e 's/gh wm/gh sr/g' \
      -e 's/\.gh-wm/.gh-sr/g' \
      -e 's/GH_WM_CONFIG/GH_SR_CONFIG/g' \
      -e 's|an-lee/gh-wm|an-lee/gh-sr|g' \
      -e 's|github.com/an-lee/gh-wm|github.com/an-lee/gh-sr|g' \
      "$f"; \
  done && \
  sed -i \
    -e 's/gh-wm/gh-sr/g' \
    -e 's|gh-wm/|gh-sr/|g' \
    Makefile .gitignore
input.description: Sweep docs, README, Makefile, templates for gh-sr rename

input.old_string:
# GitHub Workflow Manager (`gh sr`)

**GitHub Workflow Manager** is a [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) extension for managing self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across many machines from one placeâ€”usually your laptop. It connects over **SSH** (or runs **locally** with `addr: local`), installs and starts runners, and gives you a **terminal UI** for day-to-day operations.
input.new_string:
# Self-hosted runner manager for GitHub (`gh sr`)

**Self-hosted runner manager for GitHub** is a [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) extension for managing self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across many machines from one placeâ€”usually your laptop. It connects over **SSH** (or runs **locally** with `addr: local`), installs and starts runners, and gives you a **terminal UI** for day-to-day operations.

input.old_string: [https://an-lee.github.io/gh-wm/](https://an-lee.github.io/gh-wm/)
input.new_string: [https://an-lee.github.io/gh-sr/](https://an-lee.github.io/gh-sr/)

input.old_string:
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-sr/cmd/gh-wm@latest
```

The resulting binary is named `gh-wm`. To invoke it as **`gh sr`**, install the extension as above, or run `gh-wm` directly if it is on your `PATH`.
input.new_string:
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-sr/cmd/gh-sr@latest
```

The resulting binary is named `gh-sr`. To invoke it as **`gh sr`**, install the extension as above, or run `gh-sr` directly if it is on your `PATH`.

# GitHub Workflow Manager

**GitHub Workflow Manager** (`gh sr`) is a [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) extension to manage self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across multiple machines from a single control hostâ€”typically your laptopâ€”using SSH or local execution.
input.new_string:
# Self-hosted runner manager for GitHub

**Self-hosted runner manager for GitHub** (`gh sr`) is a [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) extension to manage self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across multiple machines from a single control hostâ€”typically your laptopâ€”using SSH or local execution.

input.old_string: (or the `gh-wm` binary on `PATH`).
input.new_string: (or the `gh-sr` binary on `PATH`).

input.old_string:
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-sr/cmd/gh-wm@latest
```

This installs a binary named **`gh-wm`** on your `PATH`. Use **`gh sr`** by installing the extension as above, or run `gh-wm` directly.
input.new_string:
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-sr/cmd/gh-sr@latest
```

This installs a binary named **`gh-sr`** on your `PATH`. Use **`gh sr`** by installing the extension as above, or run `gh-sr` directly.

input.old_string:
git clone https://github.com/an-lee/gh-sr.git
cd gh-wm
go build -o gh-wm ./cmd/gh-wm/
```

On Windows without GNU Make, use `go build` as above; Go writes `gh-wm.exe` when you pass `-o gh-wm.exe`.
input.new_string:
git clone https://github.com/an-lee/gh-sr.git
cd gh-sr
go build -o gh-sr ./cmd/gh-sr/
```

On Windows without GNU Make, use `go build` as above; Go writes `gh-sr.exe` when you pass `-o gh-sr.exe`.

input.old_string: | `make` / `make build` | Build `./cmd/gh-wm` into `gh-wm` (or `gh-wm.exe` on Windows when `OS` is `Windows_NT`). |
input.new_string: | `make` / `make build` | Build `./cmd/gh-sr` into `gh-sr` (or `gh-sr.exe` on Windows when `OS` is `Windows_NT`). |

input.old_string: **Linux (`--system`):** Writes `/etc/systemd/system/ghwm-runner-<instance>.service` with `User=` / `Group=` set to the SSH user. Requires the same non-interactive **`sudo`** behavior as `gh sr setup` on Linux.
input.new_string: **Linux (`--system`):** Writes `/etc/systemd/system/ghsr-runner-<instance>.service` with `User=` / `Group=` set to the SSH user. Requires the same non-interactive **`sudo`** behavior as `gh sr setup` on Linux.

content.text:
Here is what was implemented:

### Module and layout
- **`go.mod`**: `github.com/an-lee/gh-sr`
- **`cmd/gh-wm/`** â†’ **`cmd/gh-sr/`**; all imports updated to `github.com/an-lee/gh-sr/...`

### CLI and config
- Cobra **`Use: "sr"`** â†’ invoke as **`gh sr`**
- **`config.SrDir()`**, config dir **`~/.gh-sr`**, env **`GH_SR_CONFIG`**
- Copy/help strings use **â€śSelf-hosted runner manager for GitHub (gh sr)â€ť** where the old product name was

### Internal IDs
- **`ghsr-runner-*`**, **`com.github.ghsr.runner.*`**, **`gh-sr-docker-config`**, **`ghsr.invalid`**, **`::GH_SR_METRICS_*::`**, temp tarball **`ghsr-runner-...`**

### Docs and build
- **README**, **Hugo** (`baseURL`, title), **installation** / **file-structure** / **_index**, **architecture** (systemd name), **templates**, **`config/runners.yml`**, **Makefile**, **`.gitignore`**

### Verification
- **`go test ./...`** â€” all packages pass  
- **`make build`** â€” produces **`gh-sr`**; **`gh sr --help`** works

**Breaking change:** Existing setups use **`~/.gh-wm`** and **`GH_WM_CONFIG`**. Users should move to **`~/.gh-sr`** and **`GH_SR_CONFIG`**, and reinstall the extension from **`an-lee/gh-sr`** after you rename the GitHub repo.
